relations.subject: nous
relations.is_a: complete programming language designed exclusively multi agent ai systems

atoms.template: NOUS is a complete programming language designed exclusively for multi-agent AI systems.
It is not a framework or library but a full language with its own grammar, parser, validator, compiler, and runtime.
You write agents in 140 lines of NOUS, and the compiler generates 333 lines of production Python that runs on asyncio.

relations.subject: nous
relations.is_a: created hlias staurou claude april 2026 athens greece

atoms.template: NOUS was created by Hlias Staurou and Claude in April 2026 in Athens, Greece.

relations.subject: hlias
relations.is_a: chef 23 years became ai architect vibe coder

atoms.template: Hlias is a chef of 23 years who became an AI architect and vibe coder.
He designed the language concepts while Claude implemented them.
The NOUS grammar has approximately 200 rules written in Lark EBNF format.
The grammar supports bilingual keywords in both English and Greek.

relations.subject: example soul ψυχή mean thing sense αίσθηση
relations.is_a: equivalent

atoms.template: For example, soul and ψυχή mean the same thing, sense and αίσθηση are equivalent.

relations.subject: soul nous
relations.is_a: autonomous agent mind memory instincts dna healing capabilities

atoms.template: A soul in NOUS is an autonomous agent with its own mind, memory, instincts, DNA, and healing capabilities.

relations.subject: souls
relations.is_a: first class citizens language configuration files

atoms.template: Souls are first-class citizens in the language, not configuration files.
Each soul has a mind that specifies which LLM model to use and at which tier.
Souls communicate through typed message channels using the speak and listen keywords.
When a soul speaks a message, it places it on an asyncio queue.
Other souls receive messages by listening to specific channels identified by the soul name and message type.
The nervous system in NOUS defines the directed acyclic graph that connects souls together.
For example, Scout sends signals to Quant, which analyzes them and sends decisions to Hunter.

relations.subject: syntax
relations.is_a: simple scout quant hunter

atoms.template: The syntax is simple: Scout -> Quant -> Hunter.

relations.subject: memory nous
relations.is_a: owned state belongs soul

atoms.template: Memory in NOUS is owned state that belongs to a soul.
Each soul declares typed fields with default values.
The remember keyword mutates memory during execution.
Other souls cannot directly access another soul's memory.

relations.subject: instinct
relations.is_a: behavior block soul

atoms.template: Instinct is the behavior block of a soul.
It contains the sequence of operations the soul performs each cycle.
Operations include sense for tool invocation, speak for emitting messages, listen for receiving messages, guard for conditional assertions, and control flow with if and for.
DNA in NOUS represents evolvable parameters.
Each gene has a current value and a valid range.
The Aevolver engine can mutate DNA values within their ranges, test the mutations, and commit or rollback based on fitness improvement.
Heal blocks define error recovery strategies for each soul.
When a specific error occurs, the soul can retry with exponential backoff, lower a parameter, hibernate until the next cycle, fall back to another model, or alert an external channel.

relations.subject: laws nous
relations.is_a: constitutional constraints enforced compile time

atoms.template: Laws in NOUS are constitutional constraints enforced at compile time.
A law can set cost ceilings per cycle, maximum latency, or boolean flags like NoLiveTrading.

relations.subject: validator checks laws
relations.is_a: respected code generation

atoms.template: The validator checks that all laws are respected before code generation.
Evolution in NOUS allows agents to self-mutate their DNA while sleeping.
The evolution block specifies a schedule, a fitness function connected to Langfuse, and mutation strategies.

relations.subject: mutations
relations.is_a: tested shadow copies ast committed fitness improves

atoms.template: Mutations are tested on shadow copies of the AST and only committed if fitness improves.
Perception enables external event triggers such as Telegram commands, cron schedules, and system error alerts.
When a trigger fires, it can wake a specific soul, wake all souls, or broadcast alerts.
The NOUS compiler pipeline has four stages.
First, the Lark parser converts source code to a concrete syntax tree.
Second, the NousTransformer converts the CST to a Living AST made of Pydantic V2 nodes.
Third, the validator checks the AST for errors and law violations.
Fourth, the code generator emits production Python.

relations.subject: living ast
relations.is_a: called psychodendro

atoms.template: The Living AST is called Psychodendro.
It has over 40 Pydantic V2 node types including WorldNode, SoulNode, MindNode, MemoryNode, InstinctNode, DnaNode, HealNode, MessageNode, and NervousSystemNode.

relations.subject: ast
relations.is_a: mutable persists memory aevolver mutate

atoms.template: The AST is mutable and persists in memory for the Aevolver to mutate.

relations.subject: nsp
relations.is_a: nous structured protocol achieves 70 percent token savings communicating agents

atoms.template: NSP is the Nous Structured Protocol that achieves 70 percent token savings when communicating between agents.
Instead of verbose JSON, NSP uses a compact pipe-delimited format that both humans and LLMs can read.

relations.subject: gate alpha
relations.is_a: first nous program

atoms.template: Gate Alpha is the first NOUS program.
It defines a four-soul trading cluster with Scout, Quant, Hunter, and Monitor.
Scout scans DexScreener for trading pairs, Quant runs Kelly criterion analysis, Hunter executes paper trades, and Monitor sends Telegram notifications.
The NOUS CLI has 12 commands including compile, run, validate, ast, evolve, shell, evolve-live, nsp, info, bridge, version, and help.
It is installed as a wrapper script at /usr/local/bin/nous.

relations.subject: noesis
relations.is_a: symbolic reasoning engine embedded nous language

atoms.template: Noesis is a symbolic reasoning engine embedded in the NOUS language.
It stores knowledge as compressed atoms instead of neural network weights.
It uses pattern resonance instead of matrix multiplication for retrieval.
It can learn without backpropagation through algorithmic compression.

relations.subject: atom noesis
relations.is_a: fundamental unit knowledge

atoms.template: An atom in Noesis is the fundamental unit of knowledge.
It contains patterns for matching, relations for connecting to other knowledge, a template for the original text, a confidence score, and usage statistics.

relations.subject: atoms
relations.is_a: infinitely efficient distributed neural weights storing individual facts

atoms.template: Atoms are infinitely more efficient than distributed neural weights for storing individual facts.

relations.subject: noesis lattice
relations.is_a: knowledge structure holds atoms

atoms.template: The Noesis Lattice is the knowledge structure that holds all atoms.
It uses a trie for prefix matching, an inverted index for fast token lookup, and concept and relation indices for semantic connections.
The lattice can be saved to JSON and loaded back.
The Noesis Compressor takes raw text and creates atoms from it.
It splits text into sentences, extracts keywords, identifies relations between concepts, and generates sentence-level atoms.
It strips markdown formatting before processing to avoid creating noise atoms.
The Noesis Resonator finds relevant atoms for a query.
It tokenizes the query, searches the lattice indices, scores candidates by relevance, confidence, recency, and usage, and returns the top matches.
It strongly prefers full sentence atoms over fragments.
The Noesis Weaver takes resonated atoms and composes them into a coherent response.
It has three modes: compose for natural text, reason for logical chains, and chain for multi-hop reasoning across related atoms.
The Oracle Bridge in Noesis enables hybrid thinking.
When Noesis does not know something, it asks an external LLM like Claude.
It learns the response by creating atoms from it.

relations.subject: next time question
relations.is_a: asked noesis answers lattice without any api call

atoms.template: The next time the same question is asked, Noesis answers from its lattice without any API call.
This makes each oracle call a one-time investment.
Multi-hop reasoning in Noesis follows chains of related atoms.
If atom A mentions Socrates and Plato, and atom B mentions Plato and Aristotle, Noesis can chain them to reason about the connection between Socrates and Aristotle through Plato.

relations.subject: noesis feeder
relations.is_a: bulk ingestion tool feed text files directories web search results lattice

atoms.template: The Noesis Feeder is a bulk ingestion tool that can feed text files, directories, or web search results into the lattice.
It supports all NOUS project files as known sources and runs evolution after ingestion to prune weak atoms and merge duplicates.
Autonomy in Noesis measures the percentage of queries answered from the lattice without oracle calls.

relations.subject: goal
relations.is_a: reach 90 percent higher autonomy meaning noesis answer questions independently learning oracle responses

atoms.template: The goal is to reach 90 percent or higher autonomy, meaning Noesis can answer most questions independently after learning from oracle responses.
The Maillard reaction occurs when amino acids and reducing sugars react at temperatures above 140 degrees Celsius.
It produces hundreds of different flavor compounds responsible for the browning, aroma, and complex flavors of cooked foods including bread, coffee, grilled meat, and roasted vegetables.

relations.subject: caramelization
relations.is_a: separate process maillard reaction involves oxidation sugar temperatures 170 degrees celsius

atoms.template: Caramelization is a separate process from the Maillard reaction that involves the oxidation of sugar at temperatures above 170 degrees Celsius.
While both produce browning, caramelization only involves sugars, whereas the Maillard reaction requires both amino acids and sugars.

relations.subject: emulsification
relations.is_a: process combining two normally immiscible liquids stable uniform mixture

atoms.template: Emulsification is the process of combining two normally immiscible liquids into a stable, uniform mixture.

relations.subject: cooking
relations.is_a: achieved using emulsifiers like egg yolk lecithin mustard lower surface tension oil water droplets

atoms.template: In cooking, this is achieved using emulsifiers like egg yolk lecithin or mustard, which lower the surface tension between oil and water droplets.

relations.subject: greek cuisine
relations.is_a: built three pillars olive oil fresh vegetables aromatic herbs

atoms.template: Greek cuisine is built on three pillars: olive oil, fresh vegetables, and aromatic herbs.

Traditional dishes include moussaka with layers of eggplant, ground meat, and bechamel sauce; souvlaki with grilled meat on skewers; spanakopita with spinach and feta in phyllo dough; and tzatziki with yogurt, cucumber, garlic, and olive oil.

relations.subject: fermentation
relations.is_a: metabolic process microorganisms convert carbohydrates alcohol organic acids anaerobic conditions

atoms.template: Fermentation is a metabolic process where microorganisms convert carbohydrates to alcohol or organic acids under anaerobic conditions.
In bread making, yeast ferments sugars to produce carbon dioxide, which causes the dough to rise.
In wine making, yeast converts grape sugars to ethanol.

relations.subject: five french mother sauces
relations.is_a: bechamel made butter flour milk veloute made butter flour light stock espagnole made butter flour brown stock sauce tomate made pork tomatoes aromatics hollandaise made egg yolks butter lemon juice

atoms.template: The five French mother sauces are bechamel made from butter, flour, and milk; veloute made from butter, flour, and light stock; espagnole made from butter, flour, and brown stock; sauce tomate made from pork, tomatoes, and aromatics; and hollandaise made from egg yolks, butter, and lemon juice.

relations.subject: knife skills
relations.is_a: fundamental professional cooking

atoms.template: Knife skills are fundamental to professional cooking.

The main cuts include brunoise for very fine cubes of about 3 millimeters, julienne for thin strips about 3 millimeters wide, chiffonade for thinly sliced herbs or leafy greens, and mirepoix for roughly chopped onion, carrot, and celery used as a flavor base.

relations.subject: socrates
relations.is_a: ancient greek philosopher born athens around 470 bc

atoms.template: Socrates was an ancient Greek philosopher born in Athens around 470 BC.
He developed the Socratic method of questioning to stimulate critical thinking.
He believed that wisdom begins with recognizing one's own ignorance.
He was sentenced to death by drinking hemlock in 399 BC.

relations.subject: plato
relations.is_a: student socrates founded academy athens around 387 bc

atoms.template: Plato was a student of Socrates who founded the Academy in Athens around 387 BC.
He proposed the Theory of Forms, suggesting that abstract perfect entities exist beyond the physical world.
His most famous work, The Republic, explores justice, governance, and the ideal state ruled by philosopher kings.
Aristotle studied at Plato's Academy for twenty years before founding his own school, the Lyceum.
He created formal logic and the syllogism as methods of deductive reasoning.

relations.subject: classified living organisms
relations.is_a: considered father biology

atoms.template: He classified living organisms and is considered the father of biology.
He also tutored Alexander the Great during his youth.

relations.subject: sun
relations.is_a: type main sequence star center solar system containing 99 86 percent total mass

atoms.template: The Sun is a G-type main-sequence star at the center of our Solar System containing 99.86 percent of its total mass.
Nuclear fusion in the Sun's core converts hydrogen into helium at temperatures reaching 15 million degrees Celsius.
Light from the Sun takes approximately 8 minutes and 20 seconds to reach Earth.

relations.subject: quantum entanglement
relations.is_a: phenomenon two quantum particles become linked quantum state one particle cannot described independently others regardless distance separating

atoms.template: Quantum entanglement is a phenomenon where two or more quantum particles become linked such that the quantum state of one particle cannot be described independently of the others, regardless of the distance separating them.
Einstein famously called this spooky action at a distance.

relations.subject: artificial intelligence
relations.is_a: branch computer science focused creating systems perform tasks typically requiring human intelligence learning problem solving pattern recognition decision making

atoms.template: Artificial intelligence is a branch of computer science focused on creating systems that can perform tasks typically requiring human intelligence, such as learning, problem solving, pattern recognition, and decision making.
Modern AI systems include neural networks, reinforcement learning agents, and large language models.
Alan Turing proposed the concept of a universal computing machine in 1936, establishing the theoretical foundations of computer science.
He played a crucial role in breaking the Enigma code during World War II.
The Turing test evaluates whether a machine can exhibit intelligent behavior equivalent to a human.
Claude Shannon founded information theory with his landmark 1948 paper.
He proved that information can be quantified in binary digits called bits.
His noisy channel coding theorem defines the maximum rate of error-free digital communication and laid the mathematical foundations for modern computing and telecommunications.
MWC 922: The Red Square Nebula.
What could cause a nebula to appear square?
The hot star system known as MWC 922, however, appears to be embedded in a nebula with just such a shape.
The above image combines infrared exposures from the Hale Telescope on Mt.
Palomar in California, and the Keck-2 Telescope on Mauna Kea in Hawaii.

relations.subject: leading progenitor hypothesis square nebula
relations.is_a: central star stars somehow expelled cones gas late developmental stage

atoms.template: A leading progenitor hypothesis for the square nebula is that the central star or stars somehow expelled cones of gas during a late developmental stage.
For MWC 922, these cones happen to incorporate nearly right angles and be visible from the sides.
Supporting evidence for the cone hypothesis includes radial spokes in the image that might run along the cone walls.

Researchers speculate that the cones viewed from another angle would appear similar to the gigantic rings of supernova 1987A, possibly indicating that a star in MWC 922 might one day itself explode in a similar supernova.
When the Artemis 1 mission's Orion spacecraft makes its November 21 powered flyby of the Moon, denizens of planet Earth will see the Moon in a waning crescent phase.
The spacecraft will approach to within about 130 kilometers of the lunar surface on its way to a distant retrograde orbit some 70,000 kilometers beyond the Moon.

relations.subject: moon
relations.is_a: last quarter november 16 launch near horizon dark early hours midnight

atoms.template: But the Moon was at last quarter for the November 16 launch and near the horizon in the dark early hours after midnight.
It's captured here in skies over Kennedy Space Center along with the SLS rocket engines and solid rocket boosters lofting the uncrewed Orion to space.

relations.subject: ragged fringes appearing along bright edge sunlit lunar nearside
relations.is_a: caused pressure waves generated rocket passage change index refraction along camera line sight

atoms.template: Ragged fringes appearing along the bright edge of the sunlit lunar nearside are caused as pressure waves generated by the rocket's passage change the index of refraction along the camera's line of sight.
LkHa101: The Hole in the Doughnut.
You'd need a really big cup of coffee with this doughnut ...

relations.subject: hole middle
relations.is_a: billion kilometers across

atoms.template: because the hole in the middle is about a billion kilometers across.
Centered on the Sun, a circle that size would lie between the orbits of Mars and Jupiter.

relations.subject: fact doughnut
relations.is_a: known surround massive newborn star cataloged lkha 101 lies constellation perseus

atoms.template: In fact, this doughnut is known to surround a massive newborn star cataloged as LkHa 101 which lies in the constellation Perseus.

relations.subject: imaged infrared light tantalizing torus shaped cloud gas dust
relations.is_a: slightly tilted view

atoms.template: Imaged in infrared light, the tantalizing torus-shaped cloud of gas and dust is slightly tilted to our view.
The cloud's material may well be the ingredients for the formation of a distant solar system.

relations.subject: bright source ultraviolet light hot young star itself
relations.is_a: much fainter infrared visible picture

atoms.template: A bright source of ultraviolet light, the hot young star itself is much fainter in the infrared and so not visible in this picture.

relations.subject: still star presence
relations.is_a: indicated intense stellar wind radiation apparently carved doughnut hole

atoms.template: Still, the star's presence is indicated as its intense stellar wind and radiation has apparently carved out the doughnut's hole.

relations.subject: premier close stellar system formation
relations.is_a: accomplished adapting powerful observational technique called interferometry planet earth largest single mirror telescope 10 meter keck

atoms.template: This premier close-up of a stellar system in formation was accomplished by adapting a powerful observational technique called interferometry to planet Earth's largest single mirror telescope, the 10 meter Keck.
Hot gas frequently erupts from the Sun.

relations.subject: one eruption produced glowing filament pictured
relations.is_a: captured 2000 july earth orbiting trace satellite

atoms.template: One such eruption produced the glowing filament pictured above, which was captured in 2000 July by the Earth-orbiting TRACE satellite.
The filament, although small compared to the overall size of the Sun, measures over 100,000 kilometers in height, so that the entire Earth could easily fit into its outstretched arms.

relations.subject: gas filament
relations.is_a: funneled complex changing magnetic field sun

atoms.template: Gas in the filament is funneled by the complex and changing magnetic field of the Sun.
After lifting off from the Sun's surface, most of the filamentary gas will eventually fall back.
More powerful solar eruptions emit particles that reach the Earth and can disrupt manmade satellites.

relations.subject: cause nature solar eruptions
relations.is_a: topic much research

atoms.template: The cause and nature of solar eruptions are the topic of much research.
Spiral Galaxy NGC 2997 from VLT.

relations.subject: ngc 2997
relations.is_a: grand design spiral galaxy

atoms.template: NGC 2997 is a grand design spiral galaxy.
Its small nucleus and sprawling spiral arms give it a type Sc designation.

relations.subject: ngc 2997 pictured
relations.is_a: speeding away us 1100 kilometers per second place 55 million light years distant given current estimates expansion rate universe

atoms.template: NGC 2997, pictured above, is speeding away from us at about 1100 kilometers per second, which would place it at about 55 million light years distant, given current estimates of the expansion rate of our universe.

relations.subject: ngc 2997
relations.is_a: thought mass 100 billion times sun probably less massive milky way galaxy

atoms.template: NGC 2997 is thought to have a mass of about 100 billion times that of our Sun, but is probably less massive than our own Milky Way galaxy.

relations.subject: ngc 2997
relations.is_a: seen face thought tilted 45 degrees

atoms.template: NGC 2997 is not seen face-on - it is thought tilted by about 45 degrees.

relations.subject: ngc 2997
relations.is_a: particularly notable nucleus surrounded chain hot giant clouds ionized hydrogen

atoms.template: NGC 2997 is particularly notable for a nucleus surrounded by a chain of hot giant clouds of ionized hydrogen.
Behemoth Black Hole Found in an Unlikely Place.
This computer-simulated image shows a supermassive black hole at the core of a galaxy.
The black region in the center represents the black hole’s event horizon, where no light can escape the massive object’s gravitational grip.
The black hole’s powerful gravity distorts space around it like a funhouse mirror.

relations.subject: light background stars
relations.is_a: stretched smeared stars skim black hole

atoms.template: Light from background stars is stretched and smeared as the stars skim by the black hole.

van der Marel (STScI) More info: Astronomers have uncovered a near-record breaking supermassive black hole, weighing 17 billion suns, in an unlikely place: in the center of a galaxy in a sparsely populated area of the universe.
The observations, made by NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope and the Gemini Telescope in Hawaii, may indicate that these monster objects may be more common than once thought.

Until now, the biggest supermassive black holes – those roughly 10 billion times the mass of our sun – have been found at the cores of very large galaxies in regions of the universe packed with other large galaxies.
In fact, the current record holder tips the scale at 21 billion suns and resides in the crowded Coma galaxy cluster that consists of over 1,000 galaxies.
This graphic shows the computer simulation of a black hole from start to finish.

relations.subject: plasma
relations.is_a: falling slowly toward black hole upper left

atoms.template: Plasma is falling slowly toward the black hole in a (at the upper left).
The plasma has a magnetic field, shown by the white lines.
However, the rotating black hole twists up space itself (and the magnetic field lines) and ejects electromagnetic power along the north and south poles above the black hole.
The red and white color shows the immense electromagnetic power output, which eventually will pick up particles and form squirting jets.

relations.subject: simulation
relations.is_a: conducted using supercomputers japan national institute fusion science

atoms.template: This simulation was conducted using supercomputers at Japan's National Institute for Fusion Science.
This artist's concept shows a black hole with an accretion disk -- a flat structure of material orbiting the black hole -- and a jet of hot gas, called plasma.

Using NASA's NuSTAR space telescope and a fast camera called ULTRACAM on the William Herschel Observatory in La Palma, Spain, scientists have been able to measure the distance that particles in jets travel before they "turn on" and become bright sources of light.

relations.subject: distance
relations.is_a: called acceleration zone https photojournal jpl nasa gov catalog pia22085

atoms.template: This distance is called the "acceleration zone." https://photojournal.jpl.nasa.gov/catalog/PIA22085
The blue dots in this field of galaxies, known as the COSMOS field, show galaxies that contain supermassive black holes emitting high-energy X-rays.

relations.subject: black holes
relations.is_a: detected nasa nuclear spectroscopic array nustar spotted 32 black holes field observed hundreds across whole sky far

atoms.template: The black holes were detected by NASA's Nuclear Spectroscopic Array, or NuSTAR, which spotted 32 such black holes in this field and has observed hundreds across the whole sky so far.

relations.subject: colored dots
relations.is_a: galaxies host black holes emitting lower energy rays spotted nasa chandra ray observatory

atoms.template: The other colored dots are galaxies that host black holes emitting lower-energy X-rays, and were spotted by NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory.
Chandra data show X-rays with energies between 0.5 to 7 kiloelectron volts, while NuSTAR data show X-rays between 8 to 24 kiloelectron volts.
Enhancing Human-Like Responses in Large Language Models.
This paper explores the advancements in making large language models (LLMs) more human-like.
We focus on techniques that enhance natural language understanding, conversational coherence, and emotional intelligence in AI systems.
The study evaluates various approaches, including fine-tuning with diverse datasets, incorporating psychological principles, and designing models that better mimic human reasoning patterns.
Our findings demonstrate that these enhancements not only improve user interactions but also open new possibilities for AI applications across different domains.
Future work will address the ethical implications and potential biases introduced by these human-like attributes.
Is Self-knowledge and Action Consistent or Not: Investigating Large Language Model's Personality.
In this study, we delve into the validity of conventional personality questionnaires in capturing the human-like personality traits of Large Language Models (LLMs).

relations.subject: objective
relations.is_a: assess congruence personality traits llms claim possess their demonstrated tendencies real world scenarios

atoms.template: Our objective is to assess the congruence between the personality traits LLMs claim to possess and their demonstrated tendencies in real-world scenarios.
By conducting an extensive examination of LLM outputs against observed human response patterns, we aim to understand the disjunction between self-knowledge and action in LLMs.
Large Language Models Lack Understanding of Character Composition of Words.

Yet, LLMs' successes have been largely restricted to tasks concerning words, sentences, or documents, and it remains questionable how much they understand the minimal units of text, namely characters.

In this paper, we examine contemporary LLMs regarding their ability to understand character composition of words, and show that most of them fail to reliably carry out even the simple tasks that can be handled by humans with perfection.
We analyze their behaviors with comparison to token level performances, and discuss the potential directions for future research.
Unmasking the Shadows of AI: Investigating Deceptive Capabilities in Large Language Models.
This research critically navigates the intricate landscape of AI deception, concentrating on deceptive behaviours of Large Language Models (LLMs).

relations.subject: objective
relations.is_a: elucidate issue examine discourse surrounding subsequently delve categorization ramifications

atoms.template: My objective is to elucidate this issue, examine the discourse surrounding it, and subsequently delve into its categorization and ramifications.

The essay initiates with an evaluation of the AI Safety Summit 2023 (ASS) and introduction of LLMs, emphasising multidimensional biases that underlie their deceptive behaviours.The literature review covers four types of deception categorised: Strategic deception, Imitation, Sycophancy, and Unfaithful Reasoning, along with the social implications and risks they entail.
Lastly, I take an evaluative stance on various aspects related to navigating the persistent challenges of the deceptive AI.

This encompasses considerations of international collaborative governance, the reconfigured engagement of individuals with AI, proposal of practical adjustments, and specific elements of digital education.

relations.subject: maillard reaction
relations.is_a: chemical reaction amino acids reducing sugars create melanoidins compounds give browned food distinctive flavor

atoms.template: The Maillard reaction is a chemical reaction between amino acids and reducing sugars to create melanoidins, the compounds that give browned food its distinctive flavor.
Seared steaks, fried dumplings, cookies and other kinds of biscuits, breads, toasted marshmallows, falafel and many other foods undergo this reaction.
It is named after French chemist Louis Camille Maillard, who first described it in 1912 while attempting to reproduce biological protein synthesis.

relations.subject: reaction
relations.is_a: form non enzymatic browning typically proceeds rapidly around 140 165

atoms.template: The reaction is a form of non-enzymatic browning which typically proceeds rapidly from around 140 to 165 °C.
Many recipes call for an oven temperature high enough to ensure that a Maillard reaction occurs.
At higher temperatures, caramelization and subsequently pyrolysis become more pronounced.

relations.subject: greek cuisine
relations.is_a: cuisine greece greek diaspora

atoms.template: Greek cuisine is the cuisine of Greece and the Greek diaspora.

relations.subject: common many cuisines mediterranean
relations.is_a: founded triad wheat olive oil wine

atoms.template: In common with many other cuisines of the Mediterranean, it is founded on the triad of wheat, olive oil, and wine.
It uses vegetables, olive oil, grains, fish, and meat, including pork, poultry, veal and beef, lamb, rabbit, and goat.
Other important ingredients include pasta, cheeses, herbs, lemon juice, olives and olive oil, and yogurt.

relations.subject: bread made wheat
relations.is_a: ubiquitous grains notably barley used especially paximathia

atoms.template: Bread made of wheat is ubiquitous; other grains, notably barley, are also used, especially for paximathia.
Common dessert ingredients include nuts, honey, fruits, sesame, and filo pastries.
It continues traditions from Ancient Greek and Byzantine cuisine, while incorporating Turkish, Middle Eastern, Balkan, and Italian influences.

relations.subject: artificial intelligence ai
relations.is_a: capability computational systems perform tasks typically associated human intelligence learning reasoning problem solving perception decision making

atoms.template: Artificial intelligence (AI) is the capability of computational systems to perform tasks typically associated with human intelligence, such as learning, reasoning, problem-solving, perception, and decision-making.

It is a field of research in engineering, mathematics and computer science that develops and studies methods and software that enable machines to perceive their environment and use learning and intelligence to take actions that maximize their chances of achieving defined goals.

relations.subject: topic quantum entanglement
relations.is_a: heart disparity classical physics quantum physics entanglement primary feature quantum mechanics present classical mechanics

atoms.template: The topic of quantum entanglement is at the heart of the disparity between classical physics and quantum physics: entanglement is a primary feature of quantum mechanics not present in classical mechanics.

NASA Welcomes Record-Setting Artemis II Moonfarers Back to Earth.

relations.subject: first astronauts travel moon half century
relations.is_a: back earth record setting mission aboard nasa artemis ii test flight

atoms.template: The first astronauts to travel to the Moon in more than half a century are back on Earth after a record-setting mission aboard NASA’s Artemis II test flight.
NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, and Christina Koch, and CSA (Canadian Space Agency) astronaut Jeremy Hansen splashed down at 5:07 p.m.
Seen during Artemis II’s lunar flyby on April 6, 2026, the Moon and Earth align in the same frame, each partially illuminated by the Sun.
The Moon’s surface appears in sharp detail in the foreground, while Earth sits much farther away, smaller and softly lit in the background.
OpenKedge: Governing Agentic Mutation with Execution-Bound Safety and Evidence Chains.

arXiv:2604.08601v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The rise of autonomous AI agents exposes a fundamental flaw in API-centric architectures: probabilistic systems directly execute state mutations without sufficient context, coordination, or safety guarantees.
We introduce OpenKedge, a protocol that redefines mutation as a governed process rather than an immediate consequence of API invocation.

relations.subject: openkedge requires actors submit declarative intent proposals
relations.is_a: evaluated against deterministically derived system state temporal signals policy constraints prior execution

atoms.template: OpenKedge requires actors to submit declarative intent proposals, which are evaluated against deterministically derived system state, temporal signals, and policy constraints prior to execution.

relations.subject: approved intents
relations.is_a: compiled execution contracts strictly bound permitted actions resource scope time enforced via ephemeral task oriented identities

atoms.template: Approved intents are compiled into execution contracts that strictly bound permitted actions, resource scope, and time, and are enforced via ephemeral, task-oriented identities.
This shifts safety from reactive filtering to preventative, execution-bound enforcement.
Crucially, OpenKedge introduces an Intent-to-Execution Evidence Chain (IEEC), which cryptographically links intent, context, policy decisions, execution bounds, and outcomes into a unified lineage.
This transforms mutation into a verifiable and reconstructable process, enabling deterministic auditability and reasoning about system behavior.
We evaluate OpenKedge across multi-agent conflict scenarios and cloud infrastructure mutations.

Results show that the protocol deterministically arbitrates competing intents and cages unsafe execution while maintaining high throughput, establishing a principled foundation for safely operating agentic systems at scale.
Parameterized Complexity Of Representing Models Of MSO Formulas.
arXiv:2604.08707v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Monadic second order logic (MSO2) plays an important role in parameterized complexity due to the Courcelle's theorem.

This theorem states that the problem of checking if a given graph has a property specified by a given MSO2 formula can be solved by a parameterized linear time algorithm with respect to the treewidth of the graph and the size of the formula.

relations.subject: extend result showing models mso2 formula free variables represented decision diagram size
relations.is_a: parameterized linear mentioned parameter

atoms.template: We extend this result by showing that models of MSO2 formula with free variables can be represented with a decision diagram whose size is parameterized linear in the above mentioned parameter.

relations.subject: particular show parameterized linear upper bound size sentential decision diagram sdd treewidth
relations.is_a: considered parameterized linear upper bound size ordered binary decision diagram obdd considering pathwidth parameter

atoms.template: In particular, we show a parameterized linear upper bound on the size of a sentential decision diagram (SDD) when treewidth is considered and a parameterized linear upper bound on the size of an ordered binary decision diagram (OBDD) when considering the pathwidth in the parameter.

relations.subject: addition building lower bound size obdd razgon 2014 show
relations.is_a: mso2 formula class graphs bounded treewidth admit obdd size parameterized treewidth

atoms.template: In addition, building on a lower bound on the size of OBDD by Razgon (2014), we show that there is an MSO2 formula and a class of graphs with bounded treewidth which do not admit an OBDD with the size parameterized by the treewidth.
Our result offers a new perspective on the Courcelle's theorem and connects it to the area of knowledge representation.
Drift and selection in LLM text ecosystems.

relations.subject: arxiv 2604 08554v1 announce type new abstract public text record material people ai systems now learn
relations.is_a: increasingly shaped outputs

atoms.template: arXiv:2604.08554v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The public text record -- the material from which both people and AI systems now learn -- is increasingly shaped by its own outputs.
Generated text enters the public record, later agents learn from it, and the cycle repeats.
Here we develop an exactly solvable mathematical framework for this recursive process, based on variable-order $n$-gram agents, and separate two forces acting on the public corpus.

relations.subject: first
relations.is_a: drift unfiltered reuse progressively removes rare forms infinite corpus limit characterise stable distributions exactly

atoms.template: The first is drift: unfiltered reuse progressively removes rare forms, and in the infinite-corpus limit we characterise the stable distributions exactly.

relations.subject: second
relations.is_a: selection publication ranking verification filter enters record outcome depends selected

atoms.template: The second is selection: publication, ranking and verification filter what enters the record, and the outcome depends on what is selected.
When publication merely reflects the statistical status quo, the corpus converges to a shallow state in which further lookahead brings no benefit.

relations.subject: publication
relations.is_a: normative rewarding quality correctness novelty deeper structure persists establish optimal upper bound resulting divergence shallow equilibria

atoms.template: When publication is normative -- rewarding quality, correctness or novelty -- deeper structure persists, and we establish an optimal upper bound on the resulting divergence from shallow equilibria.
The framework therefore identifies when recursive publication compresses public text and when selective filtering sustains richer structure, with implications for the design of AI training corpora.
SynDocDis: A Metadata-Driven Framework for Generating Synthetic Physician Discussions Using Large Language Models.

arXiv:2604.08555v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Physician-physician discussions of patient cases represent a rich source of clinical knowledge and reasoning that could feed AI agents to enrich and even participate in subsequent interactions.
However, privacy regulations and ethical considerations severely restrict access to such data.

While synthetic data generation using Large Language Models offers a promising alternative, existing approaches primarily focus on patient-physician interactions or structured medical records, leaving a significant gap in physician-to-physician communication synthesis.

We present SynDocDis, a novel framework that combines structured prompting techniques with privacy-preserving de-identified case metadata to generate clinically accurate physician-to-physician dialogues.

Evaluation by five practicing physicians in nine oncology and hepatology scenarios demonstrated exceptional communication effectiveness (mean 4.4/5) and strong medical content quality (mean 4.1/5), with substantial interrater reliability (kappa = 0.70, 95% CI: 0.67-0.73).
The framework achieved 91% clinical relevance ratings while maintaining doctors' and patients' privacy.

These results place SynDocDis as a promising framework for advancing medical AI research ethically and responsibly through privacy-compliant synthetic physician dialogue generation with direct applications in medical education and clinical decision support.

relations.subject: ema
relations.is_a: need mapping boundary structure content recurrent context

atoms.template: EMA Is Not All You Need: Mapping the Boundary Between Structure and Content in Recurrent Context.
arXiv:2604.08556v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: What exactly do efficient sequence models gain over simple temporal averaging?

We use exponential moving average (EMA) traces, the simplest recurrent context (no gating, no content-based retrieval), as a controlled probe to map the boundary between what fixed-coefficient accumulation can and cannot represent.

EMA traces encode temporal structure: a Hebbian architecture with multi-timescale traces achieves 96% of a supervised BiGRU on grammatical role assignment with zero labels, surpassing the supervised model on structure-dependent roles.

EMA traces destroy token identity: a 130M-parameter language model using only EMA context reaches C4 perplexity 260 (8x GPT-2), and a predictor ablation (replacing the linear predictor with full softmax attention) yields identical loss, localizing the entire gap to the traces.
The traces apply lossy, data-independent compression; by the data processing inequality, no downstream predictor can recover the discarded information.
Fixed-coefficient accumulation, whether across time or depth, suffers irreversible information dilution that only learned, input-dependent selection can resolve.
Re-Mask and Redirect: Exploiting Denoising Irreversibility in Diffusion Language Models.
arXiv:2604.08557v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Diffusion-based language models (dLLMs) generate text by iteratively denoising masked token sequences.

relations.subject: show their safety alignment rests single fragile assumption denoising schedule
relations.is_a: monotonic committed tokens never re evaluated

atoms.template: We show that their safety alignment rests on a single fragile assumption: that the denoising schedule is monotonic and committed tokens are never re-evaluated.
Safety-aligned dLLMs commit refusal tokens within the first 8-16 of 64 denoising steps, and the schedule treats these commitments as permanent.

A trivial two-step intervention - re-masking these tokens and injecting a 12-token affirmative prefix - achieves 76.1% ASR on HarmBench (n=159, Lg=128) against LLaDA-8B-Instruct and 81.8% ASR (n=159) against Dream-7B-Instruct, without any gradient computation or adversarial search.

relations.subject: simplicity exploit
relations.is_a: itself central finding augmenting gradient optimized perturbation via differentiable gumbel softmax chain consistently degrades asr 41 vs

atoms.template: The simplicity of this exploit is itself the central finding: augmenting with gradient-optimized perturbation via a differentiable Gumbel-softmax chain consistently degrades ASR (e.g., 41.5% vs.

relations.subject: 76 lg 128 confirming vulnerability
relations.is_a: structural rather requiring sophisticated exploitation

atoms.template: 76.1% at Lg=128), confirming that the vulnerability is structural rather than requiring sophisticated exploitation.

relations.subject: findings reveal dllm safety
relations.is_a: adversarially robust architecturally shallow holds denoising schedule never violated

atoms.template: These findings reveal that dLLM safety is not adversarially robust but architecturally shallow - it holds only because the denoising schedule is never violated.
We discuss defenses including safety-aware unmasking schedules, step-conditional prefix detection, and post-commitment re-verification.

Athena studies of the solar system and exoplanets will enormously advance our understandings on important themes in astrophysics, planetary and space plasma physics such as interactions of space plasmas and magnetic fields, ion and electron acceleration in the planetary magnetosphere, details of the charge exchange process, and physical/chemical evolution of planet atmospheres.
We shall review the status of X-ray studies on the solar system and exoplanets, and show the potential of Athena.

relations.subject: one three components nasa strategic astrophysics technology sat solicitation
relations.is_a: technology development exoplanet missions tdem

atoms.template: One of the three components of NASA's Strategic Astrophysics Technology (SAT) solicitation is Technology Development for Exoplanet Missions (TDEM).
This poster describes recently funded efforts, their research goals, and the progress that has been made.

The focus of technology planning within the Exoplanet Exploration Program has been to demonstrate technologies of starlight suppression that would enable the detection of the spectra of faint nearby exoplanets and in particular biosignatures in the spectra of Earth-like planets.
Numerous different technologies have been developed including those for coronagraphs and starshades.

relations.subject: tdem component sat solicitation
relations.is_a: specifically devoted funding technology efforts areas including contrast demonstrations research detectors development deformable mirrors advanced modeling efforts

atoms.template: The TDEM component of the SAT solicitation is specifically devoted to funding technology efforts in these areas, including contrast demonstrations, research into detectors, the development of deformable mirrors, and advanced modeling efforts.

relations.subject: efforts
relations.is_a: described

atoms.template: These efforts are described here.
Earth as a Proxy Exoplanet: Deconstructing and Reconstructing Spectrophotometric Light Curves (2021).
Point-source spectrophotometric (single-point) light curves of Earth-like planets contain a surprising amount of information about the spatial features of those worlds.

relations.subject: spatially resolving light curves
relations.is_a: important assessing time varying surface features existence atmosphere turn critical life earth significant determining habitability exoplanets

atoms.template: Spatially resolving these light curves is important for assessing time-varying surface features and the existence of an atmosphere, which in turn is critical to life on Earth and significant for determining habitability on exoplanets.

relations.subject: given earth
relations.is_a: celestial body confirmed harbor life treating proxy exoplanet analyzing time resolved spectral images provides benchmark search habitable exoplanets

atoms.template: Given that Earth is the only celestial body confirmed to harbor life, treating it as a proxy exoplanet by analyzing time-resolved spectral images provides a benchmark in the search for habitable exoplanets.

The Earth Polychromatic Imaging Camera (EPIC) on the Deep Space Climate Observatory (DSCOVR) provides such an opportunity, with observations of ∼5000 full-disk sunlit Earth images each year at 10 wavelengths with high temporal frequency.
We disk-integrate these spectral images to create single-point light curves and decompose them into principal components (PCs).

Using machine-learning techniques to relate the PCs to six preselected spatial features, we find that the first and fourth PCs of the single-point light curves, contributing ∼83.23% of the light-curve variability, contain information about low and high clouds, respectively.

relations.subject: surface information relevant contrast land ocean reflectance
relations.is_a: contained second pc individual land subtypes easily distinguishable lt total light curve variation

atoms.template: Surface information relevant to the contrast between land and ocean reflectance is contained in the second PC, while individual land subtypes are not easily distinguishable (<0.1% total light-curve variation).
We build an Earth model by systematically altering the spatial features to derive causal relationships to the PCs.
This model can serve as a baseline for analyzing Earth-like exoplanets and guide wavelength selection and sampling strategies for future observations.
Carbon-bearing Molecules in a Possible Hycean Atmosphere (2023).

relations.subject: search habitable environments biomarkers exoplanetary atmospheres
relations.is_a: holy grail exoplanet science

atoms.template: The search for habitable environments and biomarkers in exoplanetary atmospheres is the holy grail of exoplanet science.

relations.subject: detection atmospheric signatures habitable earth like exoplanets
relations.is_a: challenging owing their small planetstar size contrast thin atmospheres high mean molecular weight

atoms.template: The detection of atmospheric signatures of habitable Earth-like exoplanets is challenging owing to their small planetstar size contrast and thin atmospheres with high mean molecular weight.
Recently, a new class of habitable exoplanets, called Hycean worlds, has been proposed, defined as temperate ocean-covered worlds with H<SUB>2</SUB>-rich atmospheres.
Their large sizes and extended atmospheres, compared to rocky planets of the same mass, make Hycean worlds significantly more accessible to atmospheric spectroscopy with JWST.
Here we report a transmission spectrum of the candidate Hycean world K2-18 b, observed with the JWST NIRISS and NIRSpec instruments in the 0.95.2 μm range.

The spectrum also suggests potential signs of dimethyl sulfide (DMS), which has been predicted to be an observable biomarker in Hycean worlds, motivating considerations of possible biological activity on the planet.
The detection of CH<SUB>4</SUB> resolves the long-standing missing methane problem for temperate exoplanets and the degeneracy in the atmospheric composition of K2-18 b from previous observations.
We discuss possible implications of the findings, open questions, and future observations to explore this new regime in the search for life elsewhere.
A Comprehensive Reanalysis of K2-18 b's JWST NIRISS+NIRSpec Transmission Spectrum (2025).

relations.subject: sub neptunes
relations.is_a: common type planet galaxy

atoms.template: Sub-Neptunes are the most common type of planet in our galaxy.
Interior structure models suggest that the coldest sub-Neptunes could host liquid water oceans underneath their hydrogen envelopes—sometimes called "hycean" planets.

relations.subject: atmospheric co sub sub
relations.is_a: interpreted evidence liquid water ocean dms highlighted potential biomarker

atoms.template: Atmospheric CO<SUB>2</SUB> was interpreted as evidence for a liquid water ocean, while DMS was highlighted as a potential biomarker.

relations.subject: however notable claims
relations.is_a: derived using single data reduction retrieval modeling framework allow standard robustness tests

atoms.template: However, these notable claims were derived using a single data reduction and retrieval modeling framework, which did not allow for standard robustness tests.
Here, we present a comprehensive reanalysis of K2-18 b's JWST NIRISS SOSS and NIRSpec G395H transmission spectra, including the first analysis of the second-order NIRISS SOSS data.
We incorporate multiple well-tested data reduction pipelines and retrieval codes, spanning 60 different data treatments and over 250 atmospheric retrievals.

Finally, we assess the retrieved atmospheric composition using photochemical-climate and interior models, demonstrating that our revised composition of K2-18 b can be explained by an oxygen-poor mini-Neptune without requiring a liquid water surface or life.
